_meta.source: TradingView FactSet Industries & Economic Sectors — USA
_meta.taxonomy: TradingView uses FactSet's Economic Sector / Industry classification — NOT GICS (S&P/MSCI), NOT ICB (FTSE), NOT SIC. Sector and industry names, groupings, and stock assignments will often differ materially from what other screeners, brokers, or data providers show. Examples: Aerospace & Defense lives in Electronic Technology (not Industrials); REITs live in Finance (not Real Estate); Media/Entertainment lives in Consumer Services (not Communications); Communications is pure telecom only. Always use TradingView URLs to verify stock classification before assuming standard industry groupings apply.
notes: All sector and industry names verified directly from tradingview.com/markets/stocks-usa/sectorandindustry-sector/*/industries/
Coal: No dedicated US-listed coal ETF exists (KOL was delisted 2020). XLE used as fallback.
Pharmaceuticals Generic: Only 1 stock on TradingView. GNRX was delisted 2019. XPH used as fallback.
Textiles: No dedicated ETF. XLB (sector) used as fallback.
Electronic Production Equipment: No dedicated fab-equipment-only ETF. SMH used (includes AMAT, LRCX, KLAC).
Forest Products / Construction Materials: No dedicated ETFs. XME / XLB used as fallbacks.
Communications sector in TradingView FactSet = pure telecom only (3 industries). Media/Entertainment live in Consumer Services.
BDRY replaced by BOAT (Apr 2026): BDRY is a freight futures pool (commodity), not a shipping equity ETF. BOAT (SonicShares Global Shipping, ~$82M AUM) holds maritime shipping equities.

CARZ replaced by DRIV (Apr 2026): CARZ rebranded to 'First Trust S-Network Future Vehicles & Technology ETF' and pivoted to EV/semiconductor ecosystem — top holdings are AMD, TSLA, MSFT, INTC, NVDA. DRIV used as the best-available auto proxy, but note that DRIV has also drifted toward EV-tech/energy (top holdings: Bloom Energy, GOOGL, TSLA, INTC, NVDA, Toyota); GM and Ford are no longer in DRIV's top 10. No clean US-listed pure-OEM ETF exists.

EATZ replaced by PEJ (May 2026): EATZ (AdvisorShares Restaurant ETF) was liquidated on May 7, 2026 (last trading day April 30, 2026) after AUM fell to ~$1.9M. PEJ (Invesco Leisure & Entertainment) is the listed fallback for Restaurants; note PEJ is broader, also covering hotels/cruise lines.

BJK replaced by XLY (May 2026): On April 9, 2026, VanEck rebranded BJK to GENZ ('VanEck Digital Native Economy ETF'). GENZ is no longer a casino/gaming ETF — same pivot pattern as CARZ. No US-listed pure-gaming ETF currently exists; XLY (Consumer Discretionary sector) used as fallback. Individual stocks: MGM, WYNN, CZR, LVS, DKNG, PENN.

SKYY methodology has changed (verified May 2026): Top holdings now DigitalOcean (6.2%), Everpure (4.3%), Oracle (4.2%), AMZN (4.1%), GOOGL (4.0%), CoreWeave (3.8%), Nutanix (3.7%), MSFT (3.3%), Akamai (3.3%), Arista (3.2%). Mega-caps are no longer capped; IBM has dropped out of top 10; CoreWeave (post-IPO 2025) is now a top holding. Still the best cloud/SaaS proxy for Internet Software/Services.

MADE (iShares U.S. Manufacturing ETF, launched July 2024): Broad manufacturing exposure (top: ETN, CAT, VRT, APH, CMI, DE, HON, PH, GM, TEL) — includes GM at 3.3% and Ford at 2.3% as OEM auto exposure. Not a pure auto-parts ETF; APTV/BWA/LEA do not appear in top 25. Best available proxy for Auto Parts: OEM but XLI is a defensible alternative.
Liquidity watch (May 2026): FTXG ~$21M AUM (stable). ONLN ~$66M AUM (slightly up from prior $58M). No imminent closure risk among remaining ETFs.

industries.name: Aerospace & Defense
industries.note: LMT, RTX, NOC, GD — confirmed in Electronic Tech by TradingView

industries.name: Electronic Production Equipment
industries.note: AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, ASML — semiconductor fab equipment. No dedicated ETF; SMH is closest.

industries.name: Computer Communications
industries.note: 14 small-cap networking companies; no dedicated ETF

industries.name: Internet Software/Services
industries.note: Cloud computing — top holdings (May 2026): DigitalOcean, Everpure, Oracle, AMZN, GOOGL, CoreWeave, Nutanix, MSFT, Akamai, Arista. Mega-caps no longer capped; methodology shifted from prior equal-weight. Best available cloud/SaaS proxy.

industries.name: Data Processing Services
industries.note: ADP, Paychex, Fiserv, Broadridge. FINX (FinTech) is wrong category.

industries.name: Life/Health Insurance
industries.note: MET, PRU, AFL — TradingView calls this 'Life/Health Insurance', not 'Life Insurance'

industries.name: Real Estate Investment Trusts
industries.note: TradingView Finance includes REITs, unlike GICS

industries.name: Finance/Rental/Leasing
industries.note: AXP, SYF, COF, CAT Finance arms

industries.name: Pharmaceuticals: Generic
industries.note: Only 1 stock on TradingView. GNRX was delisted April 2019. XPH is best available proxy.

industries.name: Food: Specialty/Candy
industries.note: First Trust Nasdaq Food & Beverage ETF (~$21M AUM)

industries.name: Apparel/Footwear
industries.note: TradingView puts NKE, LULU, PVH, HBI in Consumer Non-Durables. XLY best captures these stocks.

industries.name: Motor Vehicles
industries.note: Global X Autonomous & Electric Vehicles ETF — best-available auto proxy, but note: top holdings (May 2026) are Bloom Energy (5.0%), GOOGL (4.2%), TSLA (3.1%), INTC (3.0%), NVDA (2.5%), Toyota (2.5%). GM and Ford no longer in top 10 — DRIV has drifted toward EV-tech/energy ecosystem. CARZ (now 'First Trust S-Network Future Vehicles & Technology ETF') is even further from OEMs (top: AMD, TSLA, MSFT, INTC, NVDA). No US-listed pure-OEM auto ETF exists.

industries.name: Recreational Products
industries.note: Polaris, Brunswick, YETI, Callaway — no dedicated ETF

industries.name: Tools & Hardware
industries.note: SWK, SNA, Snap-on — TradingView name is 'Tools & Hardware'

industries.name: Automotive Aftermarket
industries.note: O'Reilly, AutoZone, Genuine Parts — missing from previous version

industries.name: Oil Refining/Marketing
industries.note: VanEck Oil Refiners ETF — MPC, VLO, PSX

industries.name: Coal
industries.note: No dedicated coal ETF exists (KOL delisted 2020). BTU/ARCH/HCC are individual stocks. XLE is best fallback.

industries.name: Oil & Gas Pipelines
industries.note: Alerian MLP ETF — ET, EPD, MPLX, WMB, KMI

industries.name: Environmental Services
industries.note: VanEck Environmental Services — WM, RSG, CWST

industries.name: Agricultural Commodities/Milling
industries.note: VanEck Agribusiness ETF — ADM, Bunge, Ingredion. DBA is commodity futures (wrong).

industries.name: Chemicals: Agricultural
industries.note: Nutrien, Mosaic, CF Industries. MOO is best available; SOIL ETF status uncertain.

industries.name: Pulp & Paper
industries.note: WRK, IP, Clearwater — no dedicated ETF

industries.name: Textiles
industries.note: Only 5 stocks on TradingView. No dedicated ETF. FTXN is Oil & Gas (wrong). XLB is sector fallback.

industries.name: Auto Parts: OEM
industries.note: iShares U.S. Manufacturing ETF (launched July 2024) — broad manufacturing exposure with auto OEM tilt (GM 3.3%, Ford 2.3% in top holdings); top holdings are ETN, CAT, VRT, APH, CMI, DE, HON, PH. Not a pure auto-parts ETF — APTV/BWA/LEA do not appear in top 25. XLI is a defensible alternative.

industries.name: Precious Metals
industries.note: Gold/silver miners — NEM, AEM, GOLD, WPM

industries.name: Steel
industries.note: VanEck Steel ETF — NUE, STLD, CLF, X

industries.name: Aluminum
industries.note: AA, ARNC — IYM is Materials ETF, no dedicated aluminum-only ETF

industries.name: Forest Products
industries.note: WY, PCH, Rayonier. No dedicated ETF; XLB is fallback.

industries.name: Wireless Telecommunications
industries.note: T-Mobile, AT&T, Verizon — TradingView Communications is PURE TELECOM only

industries.name: Specialty Telecommunications
industries.note: Satellite, fiber specialists — Iridium, Cogent, Bandwidth

TradingView_Master_Mapping._note: IMPORTANT: TradingView's Communications sector has ONLY these 3 industries. Broadcasting, Movies/Entertainment, Cable/Satellite, Media are in Consumer Services. XLC covers both, but IYZ is more accurate for pure telecom.

industries.name: Restaurants
industries.note: Invesco Leisure & Entertainment — MCD, SBUX, CMG, YUM. Note: PEJ is broader (also covers hotels/cruise lines) but is the best remaining proxy after EATZ (AdvisorShares Restaurant ETF) was liquidated May 7, 2026.

industries.name: Hotels/Resorts/Cruise lines
industries.note: Invesco Leisure & Entertainment — MAR, HLT, CCL, NCLH. CRUZ was cruise-only (wrong).

industries.name: Movies/Entertainment
industries.note: Netflix, Disney, WBD — TradingView puts this in Consumer Services, NOT Communications

industries.name: Cable/Satellite TV
industries.note: Comcast, Charter, DirecTV — in Consumer Services, NOT Communications

industries.name: Broadcasting
industries.note: Fox, Paramount Skydance (PSKY), Nexstar, Versant Media — in Consumer Services, NOT Communications

industries.name: Casinos/Gaming
industries.note: MGM, WYNN, CZR, LVS, DKNG, PENN. No US-listed pure-gaming ETF currently exists — BJK was rebranded to GENZ (VanEck Digital Native Economy ETF) on April 9, 2026 and no longer tracks gaming. XLY (Consumer Discretionary sector) used as fallback.

industries.name: Publishing: Newspapers
industries.note: NYT, News Corp — no dedicated ETF

industries.name: Miscellaneous Commercial Services
industries.note: Shopify, Verisk, Dun & Bradstreet — largest industry ($704B).

industries.name: Internet Retail
industries.note: ProShares Online Retail (~$66M AUM) — AMZN, JD, Chewy, Etsy

industries.name: Specialty Stores
industries.note: COST, TGT, TJX, ULTA, NKE retail

industries.name: Home Improvement Chains
industries.note: HD, LOW — XHB includes retail+homebuilders. ITB is pure homebuilders (wrong for this industry).

industries.name: Discount Stores
industries.note: Dollar General, Dollar Tree, Five Below

industries.name: Airlines
industries.note: Invesco Airlines ETF — AAL, DAL, UAL, LUV

industries.name: Marine Shipping
industries.note: SonicShares Global Shipping ETF (~$82M AUM) — equity-based (container, tanker, dry bulk). Holds Frontline, Mitsui O.S.K., A.P. Møller-Mærsk, Matson. Replaces BDRY (freight futures pool, not equity-based).

industries.name: Water Utilities
industries.note: Invesco Water Resources — AWK, WTRG, SJW, MSEX

industries.name: Alternative Power Generation
industries.note: Invesco Solar — ENPH, FSLR, SEDG — alt power includes solar/wind

industries.name: Investment Trusts/Mutual Funds
industries.note: Catch-all for closed-end funds, BDCs, SPACs not fitting other sectors
